title: buxus wrightii
intro: Buxus wrightii, also known as Mexican boxwood, is a type of evergreen plant native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Buxus wrightii include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Buxus wrightii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to support skin health and immune function.
